hook.text: The Niger takes a bizarre detour into the Sahara before turning south to the sea.

buildup.text: Its inland delta in Mali floods each year, creating a green oasis in the desert.

discovery.text: Timbuktu, once a legendary center of learning, sits on the Niger's great bend.

twist.text: European explorers spent centuries trying to map its strange, looping course.

climax.text: The loop exists because two ancient rivers merged into one odd path long ago.

punchline.text: A river that flows toward the desert, then changes its mind.

quiz.question: Why does the Niger River loop into the Sahara?
options: Two ancient rivers merged into one path
Humans built canals that redirected it
